commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Keep this in normal PR review until the contributor supplies real update-check/public-registry proof; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply right now.; Cleared: The diff reuses an existing workspace bounded-response helper, adds no dependency, workflow, lockfile, secret, package-resolution, or code-execution surface, and reduces unbounded network-body exposure. (sha=3f2d0425cc30f6d77332a548b7b6f4729318eee8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the public npm registry update-check `res.json()` fallback with a 512 KiB `readResponseWithLimit` read and adds adjacent mocked overflow coverage.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +24. Total +30 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the unbounded-read condition: current main calls `res.json()` on successful public-registry fallback responses. I did not establish a live memory-growth reproduction for an oversized npm response in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Keep this in normal PR review until the contributor supplies real update-check/public-registry proof; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply right now.

target.title: fix(update-check): bound npm registry JSON response reads
target.body:
## Summary

Replace raw `res.json()` with `readResponseWithLimit` (512 KB cap) in the npm registry update check to prevent unbounded buffering.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Unbounded `res.json()` in `fetchPublicNpmPackageTargetStatus` replaced with `readResponseWithLimit`. Test mock updated with `arrayBuffer` support, and an oversized-response regression test added.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 24, OpenClaw main @ 56d95b18f4

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/update-check.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  20 passed (20)
```

New test: `rejects oversized npm registry JSON responses at the 512 KB cap` — creates a streaming Response, proves the reader stops at the cap (chunks pulled < 15), and the result includes `exceeds` in the error string.

The `uses the public registry when no npm command is available` test was updated with `arrayBuffer` on the mock response so `readResponseWithLimit` can read the body.

**Observed result after fix**: All 20 tests pass including the new overflow regression test. The `readResponseWithLimit` caps npm registry responses at 512 KB before JSON parsing.

**What was not tested**: A live npm registry API call against registry.npmjs.org was not tested.

## Tests and validation

- `update-check.test.ts`: 20/20 passed ✅ (including new overflow test)

## Risk

Low. NPM package metadata responses are typically < 50 KB. 512 KB cap is generous. `readResponseWithLimit` is the standard bounded read utility.
target.author: cxbasdev

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
